title: rubus serpens var. tenellus
intro: Rubus serpens var. tenellus, also known as creeping raspberry or dwarf dewberry, is a type of thorny, woody perennial plant belonging to the rose family. The plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, particularly in its high content of ellagic acid, a compound with potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Rubus serpens var. tenellus include its ability to support immune function, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial properties, making it a promising natural remedy for various health issues. Herbal preparations of Rubus serpens var. tenellus can be found in the form of teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
